skill.name: no-bluff-seo-skill
skill.description: Enforces a verifiable SEO and GEO workflow for claims, keyword metrics, structured data, and confidence scoring.
skill.summary: Verify SEO claims with sources, confidence labels, and audit trails before publishing.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command patterns and a weak cryptography pattern, but review found only Markdown inline code and plain protocol text in SKILL.md. No executable commands, scripts, credential access, network calls, prompt injection, or malicious behavior were found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Static Findings
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged lines contain Markdown inline code for table names, metadata fields, and confidence labels, not executable shell or Ruby backtick usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown bullets and inline code labels. There is no command execution syntax, script block, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Finding
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on the description line is a false positive. The line describes SEO data sources and does not reference hashing, encryption, signatures, or cryptographic APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is front matter text describing DataForSEO and SEMRush usage. No cryptographic algorithm or implementation appears on that line.

content.value_statement: SEO copy often includes broad claims that are hard to prove. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to source claims, label confidence, and keep an audit trail.
actual_capabilities: Requires SEO claims to include a clear source.
Separates facts, forecasts, and opinions in SEO content.
Applies high, medium, low, and uncertain confidence labels to metrics.
Rejects vague vanity claims without measurable support.
Guides JSON-LD, LocalBusiness, Article, FAQ, and Review schema selection.
Defines audit trail expectations for SEO actions.
limitations: Does not include executable API client code.
Does not provide DataForSEO, SEMRush, Ahrefs, or Google Search Console credentials.
Does not verify live keyword metrics by itself.
Uses a Synthex-focused workflow that may need adaptation for other brands.

use_cases.title: Landing Page Claim Review
use_cases.description: Check page copy for unsupported rankings, ratings, and market claims before publication.

use_cases.title: Client SEO Report QA
use_cases.description: Add sources, metric types, and confidence scores to SEO insights in client reports.

use_cases.title: Structured Data Planning
use_cases.description: Choose appropriate schema types for product pages, service businesses, articles, FAQs, and reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Claim
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SEO claim and tell me if it is fact, forecast, or opinion. Add a source requirement and confidence label.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single marketing statement.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Page Copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Revise this landing page copy using the No-Bluff SEO protocol. Remove unsupported claims and keep only measurable statements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Content editing before a page goes live.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Report Insights
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these SEO observations into sourced insights. Include metric type, confidence score, and a short audit note for each insight.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Structured Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page plan for structured data needs. Recommend schema types and flag any review, FAQ, or product claims that need evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pre-launch SEO and schema review.

output_examples.input: We are the best SEO automation platform for service businesses.
output_examples.output: Reject this claim as unsupported. Replace it with a measurable statement, such as a dated ranking, customer count, or verified performance metric.

output_examples.input: A blog post includes pricing comparisons and common service questions.
output: Use Article schema for the post.
Use FAQ schema only for real questions answered on the page.
Source every comparison with a dated citation or verified dataset.

output_examples.input: Keyword traffic increased after a campaign, but the source is unclear.
output_examples.output: Label the insight as uncertain until the metric source is confirmed. Record what was checked and what evidence is still missing.

best_practices: Attach a source to every SEO or GEO claim before finalizing copy.
Use confidence labels that reflect how directly each metric was measured.
Keep an audit note that records checks, data found, changes made, and timing.
anti_patterns: Do not publish best-in-class or number-one claims without verifiable rankings.
Do not use Review schema unless the reviews are real and traceable.
Do not mix forecasts with measured facts without labeling the difference.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch live SEO data?
faq.answer: No. It defines a workflow for using sources such as DataForSEO, SEMRush, Ahrefs, and Google Search Console.

faq.question: Can it write SEO copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide copy generation when claims include sources, confidence labels, and measurable support.

faq.question: What does No-Bluff mean here?
faq.answer: It means claims must be specific, sourced, measurable, and separated from forecasts or opinions.

faq.question: Does it support structured data decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists common schema needs for product pages, service businesses, articles, FAQs, and reviews.

faq.question: Is it only for Synthex sites?
faq.answer: The source workflow mentions Synthex, but the claim verification rules can be adapted to other sites.

faq.question: What should happen to unsupported claims?
faq.answer: They should be rejected, rewritten as measurable statements, or flagged for review until evidence is available.
